title: artemisia alba subsp. suavis
intro: Artemisia alba subsp. suavis, also known as white mugwort or sweet white mugwort, is a subspecies of the artemisia alba plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving menstrual cramps, reducing anxiety and stress, and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of Artemisia alba subsp. suavis can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
